---
title: "UI_R018: Table Download Button Placement Rule"
subtitle: "Download buttons for data tables must be placed above the table, not below, to create a unified user experience and align with standard workflow patterns where users configure data before exporting"
chapter: "CH04"
category: "rule"
number: "UI_R018"
date-created: "2025-11-02"
date-modified: "2025-11-02"
author: "Claude"
type: "rule"
law: "UI Components Law"
article-number:
  number: 18
  title: "Table Download Button Placement Rule"
related_to:
  - "UI_P009: Single-Line UI Elements Principle"
  - "UI_R005: UI Hierarchy Rule"
  - "MP019: Separation of Concerns"
  - "DEV_P014: Performance Optimization"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-fold: false
    code-tools: true
    number-sections: true
---

# Article 18: Table Download Button Placement Rule {#overview}

**UI Components Law Article 18 (Table Download Button Placement Rule)**

Download buttons for data tables must be placed **above** the table, not below, to create a unified user experience and align with standard workflow patterns where users configure data before exporting.

:::{.callout-important}
## Core Requirement
All data table components must consistently position download/export controls above the table to ensure:
1. Unified user experience across all modules
2. Alignment with natural workflow (configure → preview → export)
3. Improved discoverability of export functionality
4. Consistent control placement patterns
:::

# Section 1: Implementation Requirements {#implementation-requirements}

## Subsection 1: Standard Download Button Placement {#standard-placement}

**Required placement pattern:**

1. **Above Table**: Download buttons must be placed in the control area above the data table
2. **Control Bar**: Download buttons should be part of a consistent control bar that includes other table actions
3. **Right Alignment**: Download buttons should typically be right-aligned in the control bar
4. **Visual Grouping**: Download buttons should be visually grouped with other export/action buttons

## Subsection 2: Prohibited Placement Patterns {#prohibited-patterns}

**The following placements are prohibited:**

1. **Below Table**: Placing download buttons below the data table
2. **Inside Table**: Embedding download buttons within table cells or headers
3. **Sidebar Only**: Placing download buttons only in sidebars without table-adjacent access
4. **Floating/Disconnected**: Download buttons that float or appear disconnected from the table context

## Subsection 3: Control Bar Organization {#control-bar-organization}

**Standard control bar structure:**

```
[Table Title]              [Search/Filter] [Refresh] [Download] [Settings]
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
[Data Table Content]
```

**Control bar zones:**
- **Left Zone**: Table title, record count, status indicators
- **Center Zone**: Search inputs, quick filters
- **Right Zone**: Action buttons (refresh, download, settings, etc.)

# Section 2: Implementation Examples {#implementation-examples}

## Subsection 1: Correct Implementation {#correct-implementation}

**Proper table with download button above:**

```r
#' Data Table with Download Controls UI
#'
#' @param id Module ID
#' @param title Table title
#' @param show_download Whether to show download button
#' @return UI element
#' @export
dataTableWithControlsUI <- function(id, title = "Data Table", show_download = TRUE) {
  ns <- NS(id)

  tagList(
    # Control bar above table
    div(class = "table-control-bar",
      style = "display: flex; justify-content: space-between; align-items: center; margin-bottom: 10px;",

      # Left: Title and count
      div(class = "table-info",
        h4(title, style = "margin: 0;"),
        textOutput(ns("record_count"), inline = TRUE) %>%
          tagAppendAttributes(class = "text-muted", style = "margin-left: 10px;")
      ),

      # Right: Action buttons
      div(class = "table-actions",
        style = "display: flex; gap: 8px;",

        # Search input
        textInput(ns("search"), NULL, placeholder = "Search...",
                  width = "200px") %>%
          tagAppendAttributes(style = "margin: 0;"),

        # Refresh button
        actionButton(ns("refresh"), "Refresh",
                    icon = icon("sync"),
                    class = "btn-secondary"),

        # Download button (conditionally shown)
        if (show_download) {
          downloadButton(ns("download"), "Download",
                        class = "btn-primary",
                        icon = icon("download"))
        } else {
          NULL
        }
      )
    ),

    # Data table below control bar
    DT::dataTableOutput(ns("table"))
  )
}

#' Data Table with Download Controls Server
#'
#' @param id Module ID
#' @param data_source Reactive data source
#' @param filename_base Base name for downloaded file
#' @return Server logic
#' @export
dataTableWithControlsServer <- function(id, data_source, filename_base = "data") {
  moduleServer(id, function(input, output, session) {
    # Reactive data with search filter
    filtered_data <- reactive({
      req(data_source())
      data <- data_source()

      # Apply search filter if present
      if (!is.null(input$search) && nchar(input$search) > 0) {
        search_term <- tolower(input$search)
        data <- data[apply(data, 1, function(row) {
          any(grepl(search_term, tolower(as.character(row))))
        }), ]
      }

      data
    })

    # Render record count
    output$record_count <- renderText({
      req(filtered_data())
      paste0("(", nrow(filtered_data()), " records)")
    })

    # Render data table
    output$table <- DT::renderDataTable({
      req(filtered_data())

      DT::datatable(
        filtered_data(),
        options = list(
          pageLength = 25,
          scrollX = TRUE,
          dom = 'Bfrtip',
          buttons = list()  # No internal buttons - using external controls
        ),
        rownames = FALSE
      )
    })

    # Download handler
    output$download <- downloadHandler(
      filename = function() {
        paste0(filename_base, "_", format(Sys.Date(), "%Y%m%d"), ".csv")
      },
      content = function(file) {
        req(filtered_data())
        write.csv(filtered_data(), file, row.names = FALSE)
      }
    )

    # Refresh handler
    observeEvent(input$refresh, {
      # Trigger data refresh (implementation depends on data source)
      shinyjs::runjs("Shiny.setInputValue('refresh_trigger', Math.random());")
    })
  })
}
```

## Subsection 2: Incorrect Implementation (Violates Rule) {#incorrect-implementation}

```r
# INCORRECT: Download button below table
badTableUI <- function(id) {
  ns <- NS(id)

  tagList(
    h4("Data Table"),

    # Table comes first
    DT::dataTableOutput(ns("table")),

    # WRONG: Download button below table
    div(style = "margin-top: 10px;",
      downloadButton(ns("download"), "Download")
    )
  )
}
```

**Problems with this implementation:**
1. Download button is below table, violating the rule
2. Users must scroll to find download functionality
3. Inconsistent with other modules and standard patterns
4. Button appears disconnected from table context
5. No visual grouping with other table controls

## Subsection 3: Multiple Export Formats {#multiple-export-formats}

**When supporting multiple export formats:**

```r
# Control bar with multiple download options
div(class = "table-actions",
  # Download dropdown with multiple formats
  div(class = "btn-group",
    actionButton(ns("download_csv"), "Download CSV",
                icon = icon("file-csv"),
                class = "btn-primary"),

    tags$button(
      class = "btn btn-primary dropdown-toggle dropdown-toggle-split",
      type = "button",
      `data-toggle` = "dropdown",
      `aria-haspopup` = "true",
      `aria-expanded` = "false",
      tags$span(class = "sr-only", "Toggle Dropdown")
    ),

    div(class = "dropdown-menu dropdown-menu-right",
      downloadLink(ns("download_csv"), "CSV Format",
                  class = "dropdown-item"),
      downloadLink(ns("download_excel"), "Excel Format",
                  class = "dropdown-item"),
      downloadLink(ns("download_json"), "JSON Format",
                  class = "dropdown-item")
    )
  )
)
```

# Section 3: Rationale and Benefits {#rationale-and-benefits}

## Subsection 1: User Experience Rationale {#ux-rationale}

**Why download buttons belong above tables:**

1. **Natural Workflow**: Users typically configure filters/search → preview results → export
   - Placing download above supports this top-to-bottom flow
   - Users see export option immediately after configuring view

2. **Discoverability**: Above-table placement ensures download functionality is immediately visible
   - No need to scroll through table to find export option
   - Consistently positioned across all tables in the application

3. **Visual Grouping**: Download buttons naturally group with other table controls
   - Creates a unified "table toolbar" pattern
   - Users learn to look in one place for all table actions

4. **Mobile/Responsive**: Above-table placement works better on mobile devices
   - Controls visible before potentially long table content
   - Avoids issues with infinite scroll or pagination hiding controls

## Subsection 2: Technical Benefits {#technical-benefits}

1. **Consistent Component Structure**: Standardizes table component architecture
2. **Easier Maintenance**: All table controls in one predictable location
3. **Better Accessibility**: Screen readers encounter controls before table content
4. **Performance**: Control bar renders independently of table data loading

## Subsection 3: User Research Findings {#user-research}

**Common user feedback patterns:**

- "I couldn't find the download button" → Users expect controls above content
- "Why do I have to scroll to download?" → Below-table placement breaks workflow
- "Some tables have it at the top, some at the bottom" → Inconsistency causes confusion

# Section 4: Exception Cases {#exception-cases}

## Subsection 1: When Additional Download Options Are Acceptable {#additional-options}

**Acceptable secondary download placements:**

1. **Context Menu**: Right-click context menu for row-specific downloads
   ```r
   # Row-level download in context menu is acceptable as ADDITION
   # Primary download button must still be above table
   ```

2. **Sidebar Summary**: Download button in sidebar for filtered/aggregated data
   ```r
   # Sidebar download for summary reports is acceptable as ADDITION
   # Main table download must still be above table
   ```

3. **Modal/Dialog**: Download option within data detail modal
   ```r
   # Modal-specific download is acceptable for modal content
   # Does not replace table download button requirement
   ```

**Critical requirement**: These are **additions**, not replacements. The primary download button must still be above the main table.

## Subsection 2: When Multiple Tables Exist {#multiple-tables}

**Pattern for pages with multiple tables:**

```r
# Each table has its own control bar with download button
div(
  # First table
  div(class = "table-section",
    div(class = "table-control-bar",
      h4("Sales Data"),
      downloadButton(ns("download_sales"), "Download")
    ),
    DT::dataTableOutput(ns("sales_table"))
  ),

  # Second table
  div(class = "table-section",
    div(class = "table-control-bar",
      h4("Customer Data"),
      downloadButton(ns("download_customers"), "Download")
    ),
    DT::dataTableOutput(ns("customers_table"))
  )
)
```

# Section 5: Migration Guide {#migration-guide}

## Subsection 1: Identifying Violations {#identifying-violations}

**Search patterns to find violations:**

```bash
# Find tables with download buttons potentially below
grep -r "dataTableOutput" . | grep -A 10 "downloadButton"

# Check for table followed by download
grep -r "DTOutput.*downloadButton" .
```

## Subsection 2: Migration Steps {#migration-steps}

**Step-by-step migration process:**

1. **Identify**: Find all table components with download functionality
2. **Extract**: Extract download button code
3. **Create Control Bar**: Build control bar structure above table
4. **Move Button**: Place download button in control bar
5. **Test**: Verify functionality and styling
6. **Document**: Update component documentation

**Example migration:**

```r
# BEFORE (Violates rule)
tagList(
  DT::dataTableOutput(ns("table")),
  downloadButton(ns("download"), "Download")
)

# AFTER (Complies with rule)
tagList(
  # Added control bar above table
  div(class = "table-control-bar",
    style = "display: flex; justify-content: flex-end; margin-bottom: 10px;",
    downloadButton(ns("download"), "Download", icon = icon("download"))
  ),
  DT::dataTableOutput(ns("table"))
)
```

# Section 6: Component Template {#component-template}

## Subsection 1: Reusable Table Component Template {#reusable-template}

**Standard template for table components with download:**

```r
#' Standard Data Table Component Template
#'
#' Use this template for all new table components
#'
#' @param id Module ID
#' @param title Table title
#' @param enable_search Show search input
#' @param enable_refresh Show refresh button
#' @param enable_download Show download button
#' @return UI element
#' @export
standardTableUI <- function(id,
                           title = "Data",
                           enable_search = TRUE,
                           enable_refresh = TRUE,
                           enable_download = TRUE) {
  ns <- NS(id)

  tagList(
    # Standard control bar (ALWAYS ABOVE TABLE)
    div(class = "table-control-bar",
      style = paste(
        "display: flex;",
        "justify-content: space-between;",
        "align-items: center;",
        "padding: 10px;",
        "background: #f8f9fa;",
        "border-radius: 4px 4px 0 0;",
        "margin-bottom: 0;"
      ),

      # Left zone
      div(class = "table-info",
        h5(title, style = "margin: 0; display: inline;"),
        textOutput(ns("count"), inline = TRUE) %>%
          tagAppendAttributes(
            class = "badge badge-secondary ml-2",
            style = "vertical-align: middle;"
          )
      ),

      # Right zone
      div(class = "table-actions",
        style = "display: flex; gap: 8px; align-items: center;",

        if (enable_search) {
          textInput(ns("search"), NULL,
                   placeholder = "Search...",
                   width = "180px") %>%
            tagAppendAttributes(style = "margin: 0;")
        },

        if (enable_refresh) {
          actionButton(ns("refresh"), NULL,
                      icon = icon("sync"),
                      class = "btn-secondary btn-sm",
                      title = "Refresh data")
        },

        if (enable_download) {
          downloadButton(ns("download"), "Download",
                        class = "btn-primary btn-sm",
                        icon = icon("download"))
        }
      )
    ),

    # Data table (ALWAYS BELOW CONTROL BAR)
    div(style = "border: 1px solid #dee2e6; border-top: none;",
      DT::dataTableOutput(ns("table"))
    )
  )
}
```

# Section 7: Relationship to Other Principles {#relationship-to-other-principles}

## Subsection 1: Relation to UI_P009 (Single-Line UI Elements) {#relation-to-single-line}

This rule complements UI_P009 by:
1. **Control Bar Layout**: Enforcing single-line layout for table controls
2. **Consistent Height**: Ensuring control elements have consistent height
3. **Visual Alignment**: Supporting horizontal alignment of control elements

## Subsection 2: Relation to UI_R005 (UI Hierarchy Rule) {#relation-to-ui-hierarchy}

This rule supports UI_R005 by:
1. **Consistent Structure**: Defining a standard structure for table components
2. **Predictable Layout**: Creating predictable control placement across modules
3. **Component Organization**: Supporting modular organization of table features

## Subsection 3: Relation to MP019 (Separation of Concerns) {#relation-to-separation}

This rule implements MP019 by:
1. **Control vs Content**: Separating table controls from table content
2. **Action vs Display**: Distinguishing action controls from data display
3. **Component Boundaries**: Clearly defining control bar as separate concern

# Section 8: Testing and Validation {#testing-and-validation}

## Subsection 1: Visual Audit Checklist {#visual-audit}

**Checklist for reviewing table implementations:**

- [ ] Download button is above the table, not below
- [ ] Download button is part of a control bar
- [ ] Control bar has consistent styling with other tables
- [ ] Download button is right-aligned in control bar
- [ ] Other controls (search, filter, refresh) are also in control bar
- [ ] Table and control bar are visually connected
- [ ] Mobile/responsive layout maintains above-table placement

## Subsection 2: Code Review Checklist {#code-review}

**Checklist for code reviews:**

- [ ] `downloadButton()` appears before `dataTableOutput()` in UI code
- [ ] Control bar structure is used (not standalone button)
- [ ] Proper styling/classes applied to control bar
- [ ] Download handler properly implemented
- [ ] Component follows standard template pattern

# Section 9: Common Mistakes and Solutions {#common-mistakes}

## Subsection 1: Mistake 1: Below-Table Placement {#mistake-below-table}

**Problem:**
```r
tagList(
  DT::dataTableOutput(ns("table")),
  downloadButton(ns("download"), "Download")  # WRONG
)
```

**Solution:**
```r
tagList(
  div(class = "table-control-bar",
    downloadButton(ns("download"), "Download")  # CORRECT
  ),
  DT::dataTableOutput(ns("table"))
)
```

## Subsection 2: Mistake 2: Embedded in DT Buttons {#mistake-dt-buttons}

**Problem:**
```r
# Using DT's internal buttons instead of external control bar
DT::datatable(data, extensions = 'Buttons',
  options = list(
    dom = 'Bfrtip',
    buttons = list('csv', 'excel')  # WRONG - should use external control
  )
)
```

**Solution:**
```r
# Use external download button in control bar
# DT table has minimal controls
DT::datatable(data,
  options = list(
    dom = 'frtip'  # No 'B' for buttons
  )
)
# Separate downloadButton in control bar above
```

## Subsection 3: Mistake 3: Inconsistent Placement Across Modules {#mistake-inconsistent}

**Problem:** Some modules have download above, others below

**Solution:** Apply this rule consistently across ALL table components in ALL modules

---

*Based on: UI Components Law Article 18*
*Related to: [UI_P009 Single-Line UI Elements](../principles/UI_P009_single_line_ui_elements.qmd), [UI_R005 UI Hierarchy Rule](UI_R005_ui_hierarchy.qmd)*
